item.title: That didn’t take long…
item.description: Police in Greenville, South Carolina tasing and arresting pro-choice protesters yesterday. So much for the First Amendment… Apparently the cops’ action was justified because the protest overran its allotted time: https://wlos.com/news/local/greenville-roe-v-wade-rally-picket-arrests-arrested-south-carolina-abortion-reproductive-rights-protesters-police-department-supreme-court-charges-charged

item.title: It's Time To Stop Playing Around...
item.description:
Organize, protest, volunteer, run for office. ANYTHING to stop this fucking madness!!
Be an election judge! I'm doing it for the first time this Tuesday for our primary.
As it says, you don't even have to live in the state to help these candidates!
Cool lightning pic I took last night tax
Cat tax Saucey sauces: https://twitter.com/PalmerReport/status/1540890592292622338?t=tHHMtY1rHg1XeXA3MPfFRA&s=19 - https://johnfetterman.com/ - https://timforoh.com/ - https://valdemings.com/ - https://cheribeasley.com/ - https://warnockforgeorgia.com/ - https://markkelly.com/ - https://catherinecortezmasto.com/ - https://maggiehassan.com/

item.title: I'm due for a mid life crisis any moment now.
item.description:
Today, I am 29.
I'm in the last year of my twenties, and I want to celebrate with all my elder emos the only way I know how: stolen memes.
I better make this year count. Today, I just want to blare Good Charlotte color my hair black, and immediately regret it.
Finding Emo
Both.
Stomp, clap, hey was my senior year.
I did a number on myself with the dumb tattoos I got at 18. Just waiting for a few thousand dollars to fall in my lap so I can get them covered up.
& no, neither of these tattoos are mine....but they just as easily could have been.
I locked myself in my Nana's bathroom for 2 hours after I accidentally stained her tub blue. Guess that's why it's called "Manic Panic" Maybe that's where the anxiety began.
Imma be sliding into my 30s like:
I'm adopting this murder floof shaped black void as my cat tax for this dump.
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Kettle
item.description: Protests that move quickly are harder to kettle (but are easier to split up as people get left behind). Something important to note is that kettles are used by police to gather intelligence. Police may tell you that you will be allowed to leave if you provide your name and your address, if you give information about other protestors etc. Police can and will lie to you, do not trust them. Never talk to cops. Being trapped in a kettle is stressful. Police may decide to arrest a few of you or all of you. Their goal is to gather information and to demoralize you. They might threaten or harass you. Your goal should be to remain calm and to help the people around you remain calm. Remember if nobody talks, everyone walks.
item.dc:creator: neoswhiterabbitt

item.title: bleh
item.description: Sadly, I come from one of “those” cultures where arranged marriages are normal and legal. My parents - who did nothing to help me climb out my year long depression - saw an opportunity to capitalize on my happiness and growth by deciding I am finally “ready” for marriage. It’s like when I work so hard to better myself, someone wants to knock me down again. Edit: wow, super touched by all the messages of support and even offers to move in!! My situation sucks but it’s not impossible to get out of, it just requires forcing a lot of distance on my part. Thank you all so much for at least reading my venting. Also, I’ve never received so many marriage proposals in a short period of time lol
item.dc:creator: zoraniko

item.title: At least I got that goin' for me, which is nice.
item.description: Something something silver linings...confirmed with Kaiser I can get snipped for $0, scheduling it tomorrow! My wife is visibly relieved. ETA - yes, the American "healthcare" system is a flaming manure pile fire, but at least the smoke isn't currently blowing in my face.
item.dc:creator: PballQhead

item.title: Great place to visit, and camp as well
item.description:
Repost but always yes
You wouldn't want to camp up here in the burn, but a mile down trail you're back into the green forest with a nice creek for water Peekaboo to Mt Rainier, Washington State
